name: Hikers' Trail
name_original: Bargagain – trail run circular por el Parque Natural Urbasa-Andia
description: This difficult 3.9-mile trail run in Urbasa-Andia Natural Park offers panoramic views from Bargagain peak and unique beech forests.
region_intro: Jogging around Urbasa offers diverse landscapes within the Urbasa-Andía Natural Park, characterized by extensive beech forests and expansive grasslands. The region features unique karst topography, including ravines and cliffs, providing varied terrain for running. Medium-height mountains and plateaus, with an average altitude of about 1000 meters, offer a range of elevations for different challenge levels. Natural springs like the Nacedero del Urederra and viewpoints such as Balcón de Pilatos add to the scenic appeal of the running routes. Best jogging routes around Urbasa The most popular jogging route is Hikers' Trail, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour to complete. This route navigates through varied terrain, including forest paths and open areas. Another top favourite among local runners is Bargagain – Ikuspegia Viewpoint loop from Olazti / Olazagutía, a difficult 9.5 miles (15.3 km) path. This route offers panoramic views from elevated sections. Local runners also love the Arrangarte West loop from Lizarragako mendatea, a 5.0 miles (8.1 km) trail leading through the diverse landscapes of the natural park, often completed in about 53 minutes. Jogging around Urbasa is defined by extensive beech forests, open grasslands, and varied karst topography. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Urbasa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from 10 reviews. More than 1200 runners have used komoot to explore Urbasa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Urbasa?
A: Urbasa offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 140 trails mapped on komoot. These range from easy paths to more challenging routes, ensuring options for every fitness level.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes for beginners in Urbasa?
A: Yes, Urbasa has options for beginners. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are at least 2 easy jogging routes available. For a gentle introduction, consider trails like the 'Itinerario de las fuentes' (Water Spring Itinerary) which is about 4.5 km with low difficulty, or the 'Ruta de los pastores' (Shepherds' Trail) at 7.6 km, also with low difficulty, as mentioned in regional information.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Urbasa?
A: Jogging in Urbasa offers incredibly diverse terrain. You'll encounter extensive beech forests providing shaded paths, expansive grasslands, and unique karst topography featuring ravines and cliffs. The region also includes medium-height mountains and plateaus, offering varied elevations from gentle undulations to more rugged ascents.

Q: Can I find routes with scenic viewpoints in Urbasa?
A: Absolutely! Urbasa is known for its stunning vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15006752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bargagain – Ikuspegia Viewpoint loop from Olazti / Olazagutía&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views from its elevated sections. Another notable spot is the Balcón de Pilatos, which provides breathtaking views over the Amescoa Valley and the Nacedero del Urederra.

Q: Are there any running routes that feature waterfalls or natural springs?
A: Yes, one of Urbasa's most spectacular natural features is the Nacedero del Urederra, a natural spring with striking turquoise waters, waterfalls, and pools. While access often requires prior reservation, there are well-marked trails along the riverbank that offer beautiful views and can be enjoyed for jogging or walking.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural attractions to see along the trails?
A: Beyond the stunning landscapes, you can discover various natural and historical landmarks. Keep an eye out for ancient dolmens, menhirs, and cromlechs scattered throughout the park. For natural attractions, consider exploring highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3487419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrangarte Pass&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3065525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portupekoleze Arch&lt;/a&gt;. The region's karst topography also means you might encounter interesting cave formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3006763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Los Cristinos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about jogging in Urbasa?
A: The running routes in Urbasa are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from 10 reviews. Over 1200 runners have explored Urbasa's varied terrain using komoot, often praising the diverse landscapes, from dense forests to open pastures, and the engaging challenge of the trails.

Q: Are there any moderate running routes in Urbasa?
A: Yes, Urbasa offers a good selection of moderate running routes, with 43 such trails available. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10524377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Iturmendi&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 12.3 km and typically takes about 1 hour and 22 minutes to complete, offering a balanced challenge.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Urbasa?
A: Urbasa, meaning 'humid forest' in Basque, offers a refreshing environment. The extensive beech forests provide shade, making it pleasant during warmer months. Autumn is particularly scenic with the deciduous trees displaying vibrant colors. The varied ecosystems ensure a stimulating environment throughout much of the year, though checking local weather conditions is always advisable.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging running routes for experienced trail runners?
A: For experienced trail runners seeking a challenge, Urbasa has plenty to offer, with 95 difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24649744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Itxuripunta – Bargagain Summit and Cross loop from Gazbide tumuloa&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 7.3 km route with significant elevation gain, typically taking around 1 hour and 10 minutes. These routes often feature rugged ascents and panoramic views, such as those from Monte Beriarin (San Donato), the park's highest point.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Urbasa?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Urbasa are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to explore the diverse landscapes without retracing your steps. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15006752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bargagain – Ikuspegia Viewpoint loop from Olazti / Olazagutía&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15005703" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrangarte West loop from Lizarragako mendatea&lt;/a&gt;, both popular choices among local runners.
tips: If you have come this far, the only thing left to do is contemplate the wonderful panoramic views from this viewpoint. You will be able to contemplate the entire valley to one side and the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park to the other with a superior view of the picturesque enchanted forest.

Not only are the views from the top of Bargagain wonderful, but the path to get here is also amazing. It runs through the gorge and leads to the ridge where the iron cross of the Bargagain is. This beautiful natural balcony at 1153 meters above sea level. It is the ideal place to appreciate the silhouette of Aratz to the north and the Aizkorri crest.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Bargagain trail run?
faqs.answer: This trail run is approximately 6.3 kilometers long and, given its 'difficult' rating, typically takes around 1 hour to complete for an experienced trail runner. However, allow extra time to enjoy the scenery, especially from viewpoints like Ikuspegia Viewpoint.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Bargagain trail run?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and can be challenging. You'll traverse a karstic massif with extensive beech forests and open grazing clearings. Expect rocky formations, particularly in the 'Hayedo Encantado' (Enchanted Beech Forest). While some paths are well-marked, there are steep ascents and potentially muddy conditions after rain. Sections near the Cueva del Gigante and along the crest involve rocky terrain that may require careful footing and occasional use of hands.

faqs.question: What kind of views and scenery can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers magnificent immersion in beech forests, which are a primary attraction. From the summit of Bargagain (1153 m), you'll get spectacular panoramic views of the Barranca Valley, the Aralar and Aizkorri mountain ranges, and the town of Alsasua. The 'Hayedo Encantado' section is particularly notable for its unique and picturesque rock formations intertwined with ancient beech trees, creating a magical atmosphere.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to run the Bargagain trail?
faqs.answer: The Urbasa-Andia Natural Park is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn are often recommended for trail running. Spring brings lush greenery, while autumn offers stunning fall foliage in the beech forests. Be aware that conditions can be muddy, especially after rain, and winter can bring snow and ice, making some sections more challenging.

faqs.question: Is the Bargagain trail run suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and sections requiring careful footing and occasional use of hands, this trail run is generally more suitable for experienced trail runners or hikers with good fitness and sure-footedness. Beginners might find certain parts, especially those near the Cueva del Gigante and along the crest, particularly challenging.

faqs.question: Where does the Bargagain trail run start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The specific starting point for this circular route within the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park can vary slightly depending on the exact access point. While the data doesn't specify a precise parking lot, the park generally has designated parking areas. It's advisable to check local park maps or information centers for the most convenient parking for your chosen trailhead.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees or specific permits required to access the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park for recreational activities like trail running. However, it's always a good idea to check the official park website or local information centers for any updated regulations or specific rules that might apply to certain areas or activities within the park.

faqs.question: Is the Bargagain trail run dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in natural parks are dog-friendly, specific rules regarding leashes and waste disposal can vary. It's best to confirm the current regulations for dogs within the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park before you go. Always keep your dog on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route covers significant portions of other official trails. Approximately 59% of the route follows the 'Mendizaleen ibilbidea' trail. Additionally, about 11% of the trail overlaps with both the '[CIMA NA11] Urbasa * Alsasua' and '[CIMA NA11] Urbasa * Olazagutia' routes.

faqs.question: What is a key insider tip for tackling the Bargagain trail run?
faqs.answer: Be prepared for varied conditions. While the overall distance isn't extreme, the 'difficult' rating is well-earned due to rocky sections, particularly near the Cueva del Gigante and along the crest, which may require using your hands. Good trail shoes with strong grip are essential, and consider bringing poles for stability, especially if conditions are wet or if you're less accustomed to technical terrain.
